text:
Decided to size up a bit on my #1 favourite stock last week.

After enjoying the gains in photonics and memory earlier this year with $SOI (entered at €48), $AAOI (entered at $47) and $MU (entered at $338), I've moved on to just $EOS.AX. ($GP6 is the European ticker of $EOS.AX, for diversification purposes)

EOS is becoming a full stack air defense prime. They offer sensors, AI command and control (MARSS NiDAR), kinetic effectors (RWS, interceptor drones, rocket and missile systems), and directed-energy lasers (Apollo 100 kW today, 300 kW C-RAM in development). The kill chain is combat proven in Ukraine and Iran, the order book is at an inflection point. The market still treats it like a niche Australian small cap rather than the global defense prime it's turning into.

AI / Photonics / Memory is starting to feel a little hot after a huge run, where the reward doesn't justify the risk for me personally. Defense stocks are still beaten down, even after their jump following the Trump investment news. 

Especially C-UAS focused stocks, after the Iran conflict exposed how unprepared the West still is, are structurally underappreciated. The US has spent billions on million-dollar Patriot and SM-2 interceptors trying to shoot down $20-50K Shahed drones, a cost equation that simply cannot scale. 

The Australian market has lagged the S&P 500 too, and if a deal with Iran is actually close it should benefit Australia a lot. I also believe that the next 6-12 months should bring the contract conversions that force a rerate in the stock: massive Apollo lasers, MARSS and RWS orders worth multiple billions of dollars. I expect Europe and the Middle East to be major customers in the near future.

text:
A major and largely overlooked fundamental catalyst for $SIVE is its strategic alliance with $ENAFF and O-Net Technologies.

This tripartite cooperation directly solves the biggest commercial bottleneck in silicon photonics: the highly complex and expensive optical packaging process, which historically accounted for up to 80% of total system costs.
embedded_text:
$SIVE is the most compelling CPO/photonics exposure to me.

Addressing the disinformation: I haven’t sold and don’t plan to sell a single share. 

I do think this ends up the next $80B+ $LITE one day from ~$2.1B.

And I personally have plans to acquire more ownership + support https://t.co/Mb5eRuL2N7

text:
本人持有的 serenity系列的股票：

1. $sive： Wireless 做 5G 毫米波芯片（RFIC、波束成形），Photonics 做 III-V 族化合物半导体激光器件，用于光纤通信和 AI 数据中心光互连，位于cpo上游InP DFB 激光阵列的关键供应商。

2. $hmdpf ： 加拿大电力变压器制造商。

3. $sloif： 全球 SOI（绝缘体上硅）晶圆的领导者

4.  $lpk ： 核心技术是 LIDE（激光诱导深度蚀刻），可在玻璃上精密加工通孔而不开裂，被视为下一代 AI 芯片先进封装（玻璃基板）的关键制造设备提供商。

5. $alrib ：法国分子束外延（MBE）设备制造商，生产用于化合物半导体材料生长的 MBE 系统，是 III-V 族半导体（GaAs、InP、GaN 等）生产的上游设备供应商，客户涵盖科研机构和工业客户。

6. $iqe ：英国化合物半导体外延晶圆（epiwafer）的全球最大独立供应商

7. $towa ：日本半导体封装设备制造商。

8. foci ：台湾光纤无源器件及集成模块制造商，产品涵盖光纤连接器、耦合器、WDM、有源光缆（AOC）等

9. shunsin：台湾 SiP（系统级封装）模组制造商，是富士康旗下子公司，主营无线通信模组（RF 前端/WiFi）、光纤收发模组、MEMS 传感器等的封装测试，客户涵盖消费电子和云服务器领域。

小盘的就这些吧 都买的不多。

text:
1.6T optical is the next interconnect generation, and  it’s blowing up because the rack-scale GPU/XPU clusters being built in 2026–27 finally need that bandwidth — and the volume inflection is hitting right now. 

1.6T needs 200G/lane SerDes, 1.6T DSPs, and the laser/optics (EML, CW for silicon photonics) to all be production-ready together. That convergence is happening in the 2026 window, which is why it’s inflecting now rather than gradually.

I guess demand is big enough that $CRDO will most certainly win business on this side  but it’s a new comer to optical DSP although not new to 1.6t outside optical

text:
$LITE - LUMENTUM GAVE BACK 20% FROM ITS PEAK ON ZERO COMPANY NEWS — THE OPTICS TRADE IS JUST DIGESTING

LITE fell ~10% today and is now down more than 20% from its recent high, but there's no company-specific catalyst — it's a sector-wide pullback across photonics names after a parabolic AI-optical run. Analysts still carry a Buy consensus, and demand for optical interconnects in AI data centers keeps building.

The structural case is the co-design problem: at hyperscale, moving a bit over copper now costs more energy than the compute it carries, so the interconnect flips to light. That's why co-packaged optics and external laser sources are the next bottleneck — and Lumentum sits on the laser and transceiver supply side of exactly that shift. The pullback is positioning unwinding, not a thesis break.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
Nokia is bolting Nvidia GPUs directly into its cell towers, and the reason is a number: 200 milliseconds.

A factory robot can't wait that long for a cloud server to think. So the thinking moves to the tower.

This is the edge-computing thesis finally getting concrete. Instead of routing every AI decision to a distant data center and back, $NOK turns base stations into tiny compute nodes, running the inference where the signal already is. T-Mobile was the first US carrier to pilot it. Nvidia's CEO described a future where every radio network becomes an AI network.

The latency you can't engineer away is the latency of distance.

Underneath the headline, Nokia quietly rebuilt itself. Its $2.3 billion purchase of Infinera turned it from a reseller of someone else's photonics into a vertically integrated optical company with its own chips.

Optical networks grew 20% year over year last quarter. Management raised infrastructure guidance into the teens.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

text:
❗ONLY 9 Hours Left❗

[Last Call for Substack/Full article]

Pricing update — effective June 3, 2026 (11:59PM Pacific Time)

PhotonCap started with a simple bet: that there's demand for silicon photonics research that goes deeper than press releases and earnings recaps.

That bet has paid off.

What PhotonCap does differently:

This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another.

Here's what that looks like in practice:

Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value.

Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports.

Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level.

PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean.

From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately.

Where we stand — and where we're going:

PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members.

Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider.

New pricing — effective June 3 (11:59 PM, Pacific Time):

Monthly: $50/month (currently $30)

Annual: $500/year (currently $300)

Already a paid subscriber? Your rate is locked in permanently. Nothing changes for you.

If you've been considering subscribing, now is the time. Lock in the current rate before June 3.

Content offerings, format, and frequency may evolve over time as PhotonCap grows.

— PhotonCap

text:
Sivers $SIVEF $SIVE just landed exactly the validation the thesis needed.

Its laser arrays are now integrated into $GFS ’s silicon photonics + SCALE co-packaged optics platform -> the reference designs the AI data center buildout is being built on.

This is the “light, not copper” shift made concrete. The lasers that move data inside the cluster don’t get the headlines. They get the design wins.

Still early innings but the chokepoint is real, and $SIVE just got named in it. 🔥

text:
$GFS - GLOBALFOUNDRIES CLOSED TWO AI DEALS IN ONE DAY — PROCESSOR IP AND SILICON-PHOTONICS LASERS

On Tuesday GlobalFoundries completed its acquisition of Synopsys' ARC processor-IP business — which, bolted onto MIPS, gives it a software-to-silicon stack aimed at physical AI — and separately announced a silicon-photonics partnership with Sivers to integrate laser arrays into its AI-data-center optical reference designs. Shares rose ~6%.

GF is repositioning from a commodity trailing-edge foundry into the connectivity and edge-AI layers — processor IP, photonics, and a U.S.-backed quantum foundry — where the multiple expansion lives, on top of the first dividend it declared last month.

Revenue up 157% YoY

EPS up 231% YoY

Sixth consecutive quarter of triple-digit revenue growth
Yet the stock sold off because expectations had become extremely high. (Barron's)
2️⃣ AEC leadership

Credo is widely viewed as the leader in:

Active Electrical Cables (AECs)

These products are increasingly used by hyperscalers because they:

consume less power

improve reliability

support higher bandwidth AI clusters. (Credo)

Major hyperscalers are already customers. (Barron's)
3️⃣ Moving aggressively into optics
This is the part many investors miss.

Credo recently acquired DustPhotonics to bring silicon photonics technology in-house, expanding into:

800G optics

1.6T optics

future CPO architectures

This significantly strengthens its position in the next generation of AI networking. (MarketWatch)

🧠 Why CRDO matters for the CPO theme
You recently asked about Bank of America's CPO report.
The most interesting names were:

AVGO

MRVL

LITE

COHR

I would add:

CRDO is becoming a sleeper CPO beneficiary.
Because the company now spans:
copper interconnects
optical DSPs

silicon photonics

near-package optical connectivity

Management increasingly describes the company as a connectivity platform spanning copper, optics, telemetry, and future architectures. (Seeking Alpha)

⚔️ CRDO vs peers
$AVGO
networking king
lower risk

larger company

$MRVL

switch ASICs + custom AI silicon

$LITE

optical components
$CRDO
fastest growth

connectivity specialist

highest AI networking leverage

⚠️ Risks

🧊 Valuation

The stock has already had a huge run.

Expectations are extremely high. (Barron's)
🧊 Customer concentration
Large hyperscalers drive a significant portion of demand. (Chronicle Journal Markets)

🧊 AI capex cycle

If hyperscaler spending slows:

networking names can correct sharply.

🧠 My take

Among the AI networking names, my ranking today is:

$AVGO — safest leader

$CRDO — highest growth

$MRVL — strongest upside optionality

$LITE — optics leader

If your thesis is:

AI clusters continue getting bigger,

then networking may become as important as GPUs.

And CRDO is one of the purest ways to play that trend. (Credo)

Bottom line
$CRDO is no longer just a cable company.
It is becoming:
an AI connectivity platform spanning AECs, optics, silicon photonics, and next-generation AI networking.

For investors looking beyond GPUs, CRDO is one of the most important names to watch in the AI data movement layer. (Credo)

Dr. Bastani is a veteran executive in the global semiconductor industry with over 40 years of operational and board-level experience. One of his current responsibilities includes:

Sivers Semiconductors AB: He joined as a Strategic Advisor to the Board and Management team in August 2023. In early 2024, he was appointed US Executive Chairman of Sivers Semiconductors Inc. and nominated as Chairman of the Board of Directors for the Swedish parent company, Sivers Semiconductors AB. His mandate focuses on accelerating the company's US growth strategy following its acquisition of Mixcomm (a mmWave 5G/Satcom specialist).

P.S:  2015 – 2023 Dr. Bastani worked for GlobalFoundries as the Senior VP & GM, Mobile & Wireless Infrastructure; later Senior Advisor to the CEO of GF

Many people read the Sivers $SIVE/GlobalFoundries $GFS PR and see "AI optics."

I see something more: GF's SCALE platform is explicitly positioned as a CPO solution for OCI (Optical Scale-Up Initiative), whose founding members are: NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI

Hennce, Sivers $SIVE just became the first publicly named laser partner integrated into SCALE reference designs. GF CPO solution for OCI. SCALE is the Industry First MSA capable CPO platform. 

OCI drives the transition from 800G EMLs to 1.6T/3.2T SiPh + CW laser architectures, this partnership hence is highly strategically important.

Not just a partnership announcement, it is an ecosystem announcement.

text:
Let me explain what the GlobalFoundries deal actually does for $SIVE — because most retail is reading "partnership" and missing the structural significance.

Reference designs are how foundry ecosystems work.
When a chip designer wants to build silicon photonics (let's say AMD, Broadcom, or a hyperscaler ASIC team), they don't start from scratch. They start with the foundry's reference design — a pre-validated blueprint for how the components fit together.
GlobalFoundries' silicon photonics platform is one of the most strategically positioned in the industry. Their SCALE™ optical engine is the foundation for next-generation co-packaged optics.

$SIVE's lasers are now embedded in that reference design.

What this means in practice:

→ Every customer who designs silicon photonics on GF's platform starts with $SIVE lasers as the default light source
→ Switching to a non-$SIVE laser requires custom engineering work, custom validation, and supply chain risk
→ $SIVE has effectively become the path of least resistance for the entire GF silicon photonics customer base

The math of why this is different from a normal customer win:
A typical customer deal = one company buys $SIVE lasers.

A foundry reference design = every customer using that foundry's silicon photonics platform encounters $SIVE lasers in their default design flow.

One captures one customer. The other captures an ecosystem.

The competitive context:
Coherent, Lumentum, MACOM — these are the larger optical/photonics names retail compares $SIVE to. None of them just got embedded in a major foundry's silicon photonics reference design at the laser layer.

$SIVE just did something its bigger competitors did not.
And the addressable market GF is targeting? $25B in pluggable optics by 2030.

This is the moat people argued $SIVE didn't have. It just got built. $SIVE

Reference designs are how foundry ecosystems work. When a chip designer wants to build silicon photonics (let's say AMD, Broadcom, or a hyperscaler ASIC team), they don't start from scratch. They start with the foundry's reference design — a pre-validated blueprint for how the components fit together. GlobalFoundries' silicon photonics platform is one of the most strategically positioned in the industry. Their SCALE™ optical engine is the foundation for next-generation co-packaged optics. $SIVE's lasers are now embedded in that reference design. What this means in practice: → Every customer who designs silicon photonics on GF's platform starts with $SIVE lasers as the default light source → Switching to a non-$SIVE laser requires custom engineering work, custom validation, and supply chain risk → $SIVE has effectively become the path of least resistance for the entire GF silicon photonics customer base The math of why this is different from a normal customer win: A typical customer deal = one company buys $SIVE lasers. A foundry reference design = every customer using that foundry's silicon photonics platform encounters $SIVE lasers in their default design flow. One captures one customer. The other captures an ecosystem. The competitive context: Coherent, Lumentum, MACOM — these are the larger optical/photonics names retail compares $SIVE to. None of them just got embedded in a major foundry's silicon photonics reference design at the laser layer. $SIVE just did something its bigger competitors did not. And the addressable market GF is targeting? $25B in pluggable optics by 2030. This is the moat people argued $SIVE didn't have. "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjE5MjEwMDk0NTY4NjUyODA=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "$DBO.TO D-BOX Continues Growth Trajectory Surpassing 1,200 Active Screens and Generating Fourth Quarter Adjusted EBITDA of $3.2 Million Fueled by $3.0 Million of Royalties “D-BOX delivered exceptional fourth quarter and fiscal 2026 results, reflecting continued execution across our theatrical focused growth strategy” said Naveen Prasad, CEO of D-BOX. “During the year, we achieved a record expansion of our theatrical footprint by adding 189 net new screens, taking us to 1,201 active theatrical screens globally. This growth reflects increasing recognition from our exhibitor partners that D-BOX is a highly deployable premium experience that can help maximize per-patron yield by delivering a uniquely immersive experience for audiences of all ages. Our total revenues grew by 35% year-over-year to $57.6 million, while adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $15.5 million. Net profit before income taxes surged 193% to $11.4 million. These robust results have further strengthened our balance sheet and enhanced our financial flexibility, positioning D-BOX to offer innovative financing solutions and capitalize on strategic opportunities as we move forward.” Q4 2026 Operating Results Fourth quarter royalty revenues increased 33% to $3.0 million compared with $2.2 million in the prior year. This year‑over‑year increase was driven primarily by a 24.8%1 increase in the North American domestic box office, and the 18.7% expansion in our installed footprint. For the second consecutive quarter, the gross domestic box office remained less concentrated across a broader range of films, reflective of the seasonality and composition of the release calendar during the period, rather than being dominated by blockbuster titles, which are typically more favorable for D-BOX. D-BOX-encoded movies themselves delivered strong results among the highest grossing titles of the quarter, including Project Hail Mary, Avatar: Fire and Ash and Hoppers. System sales to theatrical customers increased 682% to $7.8 million compared with $1.0 million last year. This increase reflects D-BOX’s emergence as a preferred capital allocation priority for it’s exhibitors partners looking to maximize per-patron yield. Seventy four new screens were installed in the quarter, offset by 8 deactivations, compared to 6 net new screens from the same period last year. While this surge highlights the D-BOX brand’s growing market penetration and the impact of high-performing blockbuster content, we continue to maintain a disciplined outlook regarding future hardware cycles. We expect system sales to normalize toward historical patterns of seasonality, as exhibitor capital expenditures typically fluctuate based on our exhibitor partners' positioning for the strength and timing of the global box office slate and their corresponding free cash flow generation. The Company ended the year with a significantly improved financial position: cash and cash equivalents increased to $17.6 million (up from $7.8 million), working capital rose to $24.7 million, and equity nearly doubled to $34.6 million. These improvements materially strengthen the Company's financial flexibility provide a solid foundation for continued investing in innovation, operational excellence, and strategic exhibitor financing and deployment initiatives. The strengthened balance sheet also supports disciplined capital allocation initiatives focused on creating long term shareholder value, including initiatives such as the Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB). Additionally, the Company recorded a deferred tax asset (DTA) of $6.4 million, relating to the recognition of previously unused tax losses and unused tax credits. This recognition is based on the expectation that future taxable profits will be available to utilize these losses and credits, supported by the Company’s recent history of success and positive outlook for future cash flows and profitability. Net profit for the quarter reached $1.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA2 totaled $3.2 million, representing a 22% Adjusted EBITDA margin², up 105% year-over-year and demonstrating continued focus on cost control and operational efficiency. Given the inherent variability and seasonality of quarterly sales, we continue to emphasize the importance of assessing the Company’s performance on a trailing twelve-month basis. FY26 Operating Results Total revenues increased by 35% year-over-year to $57.6 million, with adjusted EBITDA more than doubling to $15.5 million and net profit before income taxes rising 193% to $11.4 million. The Company’s strong top-line growth was driven primarily by a surge in theatrical system sales (up 133% for the year) and recurring rights for use, rental, and maintenance revenues (up 32%). This performance underscores the effectiveness of D-BOX’s commercial strategy and its ability to scale profitably. D-BOX continued to outperform the North American domestic box office on an annualized basis, with the box office increasing 7%3 while D-BOX royalties rose 32%. Our base of motion-enabled screens grew by 18.7% year-over-year, reaching 1,201 active screens. D-BOX’s technology is increasingly recognized as a premium upgrade by exhibitors, enabling them to maximize per-patron yield and deliver unique, immersive experiences. The expansion of the installed footprint directly supports high-margin, recurring royalty and maintenance revenues, which have proven resilient and outperformed broader box office trends. This continued outperformance reinforces D-BOX’s growing position within the premium theatrical ecosystem and further supports the long-term scalability of its recurring revenue model. Although revenues from sim racing and simulation and training system sales declined by 10% and 16% respectively, these segments continue to play a strategically important role within D-BOX’s broader commercial platform and overall profitability profile. Both customer groups utilize the same core motion technology and components as our theatrical clients, which allows D-BOX to leverage economies of scale across its operations. By sharing operating overhead and production resources, these segments help optimize cost efficiency and enhance profitability, even during periods of lower sales. This synergy not only mitigates risk but also improves operating leverage, enhances production scale efficiencies, and preserves D-BOX’s ability to capitalize on growth opportunities across multiple end markets."}}}, "rest_id": "2061921009972744328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3976", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2061924590696185880", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2472", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Portfolio Update (June 2026): +158.57% (YTD)

1. $SIVE (40%) - CPO / 1.6T for Photonics

I didn’t touch $SIVE this month. I held my position and let the market do the work. It was a 30% allocation, but after a 75% surge over the last 30 days, it grew to 40%.

The thesis is simple, and my conviction has only deepened over the last few months. People are cheering for $10, but they are missing the bigger picture: we haven’t even seen what 2027 looks like yet. Or 2028! 

There is already structural validation across the board with hyperscalers, OEMs, $AAPL, $AEVA, and US defense supply chains. Add factors like the institutional reports, $GFS tech validation, the upcoming US listing, Chips Act, MOU... 

Almost lost count of how many catalysts we got. 😅

$NVDA needs 20x more InP laser capacity by 2030, and current suppliers can only address 60% of that. Guess what? $SIVE comes to the rescue!

Will look to keep accumulating during decent entries throughout H2'26. 

2. $NBIS (35%) - AI Cloud for Compute

Up from 30% to 35% of my portfolio. This is a long-term compounder. The compute demand is astronomical, and their world-class technology and software integration set them apart. 

Management completely won me over. I see this as a $100B company by year-end. If anyone is positioned to become the 4th major hyperscaler, it is Nebius. 

With major catalysts lined up for H2 2026, selling - or even trimming - would be absolutely criminal in my view.

Also it's Jensen's favorite child. 😆

3. $SMCI & $PENG (10% overall) - AI Servers

Both ride the massive AI server trend (heading to $100 soon), but with distinct technological edges.

$PENG caught my eye because of its immense upside in the memory and photonics sectors, which will take their margins to a completely new level compared to today.

$SMCI is setting up for a $DELL like run. Trading at less than 1x sales? It is a no-brainer. AI server demand is compounding, and only 40% of planned US data centers are currently operational. Once the export-control narrative fades, the upside is massive - especially with Elon (SpaceX, xAI, Tesla) as a client. 

4. $TRT (5%) - Back-end Testing

ATMP is still an underlooked sector in the AI infrastructure buildout with many hidden goldmines. 

$TRT looks great to capitalize on the critical need for chip testing across multiple sectors. When demand vastly outstrips supply, the margin for low-quality chips shrinks to zero.

They are posting triple-digit growth, and margins will aggressively expand through 2027 and 2028 as new orders flood in. 

The current upside is driven by CPU/GPUs (with $AMD as the likely customer) and Power Semi, but I see them expanding into Memory and Photonics as well. 

Capacity expansion is a massive bullish signal, and their balance sheet is pristine (0.1 D/E). 

I see this easily breaking $50 by the end of the year, or H1 2027 at the latest.

5. $AAOI & $MU (5%)

$MU is a multi-month swing trade for me, riding the road to a $2T/$3T valuation backed by the American Dream memory narrative.

With $AAOI, I am still evaluating. I might swing it to $300, or I might accumulate more as a long position, because this definitely looks like a $750 stock by 2028. 

Both are tied to massive, sector-specific demand ramps, with $AAOI in transceiver sales and $MU with HBM. 

6. Cash (5%)

I am actually proud of maintaining a decent cash position. Had I not bought $MU, $AAOI, $SMCI, it would have been around 10%. 

This is giving me the optionality to buy the dip on my core holdings if they hit key levels, or to execute new swing setups when I see the right entry.

Things I did this month:

> Sold $KRKNF & $ZETA: Exited these positions to reallocate into higher-conviction plays within the core AI Infrastructure theme. Both remain great longs in my view, but this was purely a pivot for better short-term upside.

> Locked in some massive gains on $SOI, $AAOI, and $TSEM. However, I ended up getting back into $AAOI after the market handed me a $150 entry - an absolute gift.

> $SOI, $AAOI and $TSEM had also given me huge gains and so I decided to sell them. Bought $AAOI again because of the $150 entry, which was a gift. 

Looking ahead: 

> Now that I got memory exposure (last month's goal), my research is shifting toward Energy and Power Semi names. Looking to potentially add one or two high-conviction companies here next.

> I’m intentionally sitting out of Space stocks for now. While I wanted exposure last month, spreading myself too thin risks me loosing focus. Definitely don't want that. 

Make sure you keep doing your own research and building conviction. We already have many soft hands in this market. You don't need to be another one!

- AI

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjE5MjMwNDg2Njg5NDIzMzY=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Portfolio Update (June 2026): +158.57% (YTD) 1. $SIVE (40%) - CPO / 1.6T for Photonics I didn’t touch $SIVE this month. I held my position and let the market do the work. It was a 30% allocation, but after a 75% surge over the last 30 days, it grew to 40%. The thesis is simple, and my conviction has only deepened over the last few months. People are cheering for $10, but they are missing the bigger picture: we haven’t even seen what 2027 looks like yet. Or 2028! There is already structural validation across the board with hyperscalers, OEMs, $AAPL, $AEVA, and US defense supply chains. Add factors like the institutional reports, $GFS tech validation, the upcoming US listing, Chips Act, MOU... Almost lost count of how many catalysts we got. 😅 $NVDA needs 20x more InP laser capacity by 2030, and current suppliers can only address 60% of that. Guess what? $SIVE comes to the rescue! Will look to keep accumulating during decent entries throughout H2'26. 2. $NBIS (35%) - AI Cloud for Compute Up from 30% to 35% of my portfolio. This is a long-term compounder. The compute demand is astronomical, and their world-class technology and software integration set them apart. Management completely won me over. I see this as a $100B company by year-end. If anyone is positioned to become the 4th major hyperscaler, it is Nebius. With major catalysts lined up for H2 2026, selling - or even trimming - would be absolutely criminal in my view. Also it's Jensen's favorite child. 😆 3. $SMCI & $PENG (10% overall) - AI Servers Both ride the massive AI server trend (heading to $100 soon), but with distinct technological edges. $PENG caught my eye because of its immense upside in the memory and photonics sectors, which will take their margins to a completely new level compared to today. $SMCI is setting up for a $DELL like run. Trading at less than 1x sales? It is a no-brainer. AI server demand is compounding, and only 40% of planned US data centers are currently operational. Once the export-control narrative fades, the upside is massive - especially with Elon (SpaceX, xAI, Tesla) as a client. 4. $TRT (5%) - Back-end Testing ATMP is still an underlooked sector in the AI infrastructure buildout with many hidden goldmines. $TRT looks great to capitalize on the critical need for chip testing across multiple sectors. When demand vastly outstrips supply, the margin for low-quality chips shrinks to zero. They are posting triple-digit growth, and margins will aggressively expand through 2027 and 2028 as new orders flood in. The current upside is driven by CPU/GPUs (with $AMD as the likely customer) and Power Semi, but I see them expanding into Memory and Photonics as well. Capacity expansion is a massive bullish signal, and their balance sheet is pristine (0.1 D/E). I see this easily breaking $50 by the end of the year, or H1 2027 at the latest. 5. $AAOI & $MU (5%) $MU is a multi-month swing trade for me, riding the road to a $2T/$3T valuation backed by the American Dream memory narrative. With $AAOI, I am still evaluating. I might swing it to $300, or I might accumulate more as a long position, because this definitely looks like a $750 stock by 2028. Both are tied to massive, sector-specific demand ramps, with $AAOI in transceiver sales and $MU with HBM. 6. Cash (5%) I am actually proud of maintaining a decent cash position. Had I not bought $MU, $AAOI, $SMCI, it would have been around 10%. This is giving me the optionality to buy the dip on my core holdings if they hit key levels, or to execute new swing setups when I see the right entry. Things I did this month: > Sold $KRKNF & $ZETA: Exited these positions to reallocate into higher-conviction plays within the core AI Infrastructure theme. Both remain great longs in my view, but this was purely a pivot for better short-term upside. > Locked in some massive gains on $SOI, $AAOI, and $TSEM. However, I ended up getting back into $AAOI after the market handed me a $150 entry - an absolute gift. > $SOI, $AAOI and $TSEM had also given me huge gains and so I decided to sell them. Bought $AAOI again because of the $150 entry, which was a gift. Looking ahead: > Now that I got memory exposure (last month's goal), my research is shifting toward Energy and Power Semi names. Looking to potentially add one or two high-conviction companies here next. > I’m intentionally sitting out of Space stocks for now. While I wanted exposure last month, spreading myself too thin risks me loosing focus. Definitely don't want that. Make sure you keep doing your own research and building conviction. We already have many soft hands in this market. You don't need to be another one! - AI"}}}, "rest_id": "2061923049142886527", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "49274", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

10) SUBSTRATE / WAFER
The base wafers everything is grown on — InP/GaAs for lasers, SOI for silicon photonics.

$AXTI · $SLOIF (Soitec) · JP: Sumitomo, JX, Shin-Etsu, SUMCO · DE: Freiberger

7) InP LASER / EML
The light source. Silicon can't emit light — III-V indium phosphide does the lasing. The hardest physical piece.

$COHR (owns its InP fab) · $LITE · $AAOI · EU: Sivers, EFFECT Photonics · JP: Sumitomo, Mitsubishi · TW: LandMark

6) SiPho OPTICAL ENGINE (PIC)
Silicon-photonics chip integrating modulators/waveguides — the heart of a modern module / CPO.

$CRDO (DustPhotonics) · $COHR · $POET · Intel/Jabil · Lightmatter, Ayar (private)

text:
🚀 8 ETF Đang Xây Dựng Tương Lai Của AI

AI không chỉ là NVIDIA hay ChatGPT. Đằng sau là cả một hệ sinh thái gồm chip, bộ nhớ, điện năng, quang tử, robot và không gian.

💡 FOTO — Quang tử & truyền dữ liệu AI
“Hệ thần kinh” của AI với laser, silicon photonics và hạ tầng kết nối tốc độ cao.

🧠 DRAM — Bộ nhớ AI
Đặt cược vào HBM, NAND và các công ty lưu trữ dữ liệu cho kỷ nguyên AI.

⚛️ NLR — Năng lượng hạt nhân
Nguồn điện dài hạn cho các trung tâm dữ liệu và siêu máy tính AI.

🚀 NASA — Kinh tế không gian
Vệ tinh, tên lửa và hạ tầng cho nền kinh tế ngoài Trái Đất.

🤖 HUMN — Robot & AI hiện thân
Đầu tư vào robot hình người và tự động hóa thế hệ mới.

🔬 EUV — Thiết bị chế tạo chip
Những cỗ máy tạo ra các con chip AI tiên tiến nhất thế giới.

💻 SMH — Bán dẫn toàn cầu
Tiếp cận toàn bộ chuỗi giá trị ngành chip.

⚡ GRID — Hạ tầng điện
Các công ty nâng cấp lưới điện để đáp ứng nhu cầu năng lượng bùng nổ.

🎯 Nếu AI là một cơ thể:
🧠 DRAM = Trí nhớ
💻 SMH = Bộ não
🔬 EUV = Nhà máy sản xuất chip
💡 FOTO = Hệ thần kinh
⚡ GRID = Hệ tuần hoàn năng lượng
⚛️ NLR = Nguồn điện
🤖 HUMN = Cơ thể robot
🚀 NASA = Không gian mở rộng tương lai

Đây là 8 chủ đề đầu tư đang nằm ở trung tâm của siêu chu kỳ AI trong thập kỷ tới.

text:
💡 $FOTO CHÍNH THỨC NIÊM YẾT – ETF ĐẦU TIÊN TẬP TRUNG VÀO TOÀN BỘ CHUỖI GIÁ TRỊ QUANG TỬ

Nếu chip là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh giúp dữ liệu di chuyển với tốc độ ánh sáng.

🚀 $FOTO (First Trust Indxx Global Photonics ETF) đầu tư vào các công ty hàng đầu trong lĩnh vực:

🔹 Truyền thông quang (Optical Networking)
🔹 Laser công suất cao
🔹 Silicon Photonics
🔹 Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học

🏆 Các khoản nắm giữ lớn nhất:
• Lumentum ($LITE) – Laser & module quang cho AI Datacenter
• IPG Photonics ($IPGP) – Laser công nghiệp hàng đầu thế giới
• Coherent ($COHR) – Chuỗi giá trị quang tử toàn diện
• Ciena ($CIEN) – Hạ tầng mạng quang tốc độ cao
• nLIGHT ($LASR) – Laser hiệu suất cao
• Fabrinet ($FN) – Nhà sản xuất module quang quy mô lớn
• AAOI ($AAOI) – Tích hợp dọc trong chuỗi cung ứng quang học

📊 Phân bổ ngành:
• 40.7% Thiết bị truyền thông quang & module
• 26.1% Laser
• 17.3% Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học
• 9.0% Gia công sản xuất quang học
• 6.9% Khác

🔥 Vì sao quang tử là chủ đề đáng chú ý?

✅ AI tạo ra lượng dữ liệu khổng lồ
✅ GPU ngày càng mạnh nhưng kết nối đang trở thành nút thắt cổ chai
✅ Silicon Photonics giúp tăng băng thông và giảm điện năng tiêu thụ
✅ Các ông lớn AI đang đẩy mạnh đầu tư vào hạ tầng quang học cho Datacenter thế hệ mới

🎯 Nếu NVIDIA là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh kết nối toàn bộ cơ thể AI.

Sherry thấy đây là một trong những chủ đề hạ tầng AI đáng theo dõi nhất trong giai đoạn 2025–2030 khi nhu cầu truyền dữ liệu tốc độ cao tiếp tục bùng nổ.

📌 Nghiên cứu kỹ trước khi đầu tư. Bài viết chỉ mang tính chất tham khảo, không phải khuyến nghị mua bán.
Sherry Lab 🚀

text:
Hey chat, let me put some respect on $ALRIB 🧵

Riber SA. French semi equipment maker. ~€300M market cap. 109 employees. global leader in MBE — the process used to grow semiconductor layers inside quantum dot lasers, photonics chips, and AI data center components.

You've never heard of them. that's the point.
The numbers→ revenue €40.3M, net income €5.2M, margin 13% → order book up 24% to €26.9M end of 2025 → fresh Japan production order in Jan 2026 (not even in the book yet)

ROSIE is the real thesis - Their new platform for silicon-based integrated photonics, compatible with 300mm production lines. first two units ordered in 2025. ROSIE 2 (dual-chamber) in production in 2026.

This is the format hyperscalers need. 300mm compatibility = plug into existing fab infrastructure. that's not a research tool. that's an industrial product entering a massive market.

Why it's undervalued - Every quantum dot laser in every AI data center optical link needs MBE - Riber is the global leader in that equipment. 
One company : 109 people, €300M market cap. analyst target still €6. stock at €15.70. analysts haven't moved since the AI photonics cycle started.

Upcoming catalyst June 17 shareholder meeting. €0.10/share cash distribution (+25%) proposed, payable June 24. Profitable company returning cash while deploying next-gen tech. 

This is what undervalued looks like before the market figures it out.

text:
Global semiconductor equipment sales, the lithography, metrology, and photonics tools that build every chip, are projected to hit a record $133 billion in 2025 and $156 billion by 2027. 

Every AI breakthrough traces back to a fab, and every fab traces back to the equipment that builds it, which is exactly where this spending lands. 

Source: SEMI projections, December 2025

text:
翻译

$SIVE(Sivers Semiconductors)是我心目中最具吸引力的 CPO / 光子学(photonics)敞口标的。

回应一下虚假信息:我一股都没卖,也不打算卖任何一股。

我确实认为这家公司有一天会从现在约 21 亿美元市值,成长为下一个 800 亿美元以上的 $LITE(Lumentum)。

而且我个人有计划继续增持,并支持他们的并购(M&A)前景。

我相信财报电话会议纪要(earnings transcripts)将非常正面。

因为在过去几个月里,我们已经陆续发现以下信息:

AlChip(世芯)/ Amazon(亚马逊)的定向增发,对 Ayar Labs 是利好 → 对 $SIVE 而言意味着被设计进 Trainium 4(亚马逊第四代自研 AI 芯片)。

纬颖(Wiwynn)+ Ayar 的 CPO 放量。

$JBL(捷普 / Jabil)1.6T 光收发器爬坡,Sivers 的进展比市场预期更快(具备相对显著的护城河 + 有多少产能客户就要多少需求)。

O-Net(昂纳科技)与 $SIVE 一起扩大 ELS(外部光源)工作规模。
$YSS(York Space Systems)收购了 $SIVE 的 Allspace 主要合作伙伴,正把 Sivers 设计进太空国防主承包商(Space defense primes)。

$SIVE 获得新的 CHIPS 法案(CHIPS ACT)资助。

$POET(POET Technologies)下半年放量,以及他们新拿到的从 5,000 万美元上调到 5 亿美元的订单(以 $SIVE 作为光源)。

信息发现:$AAPL(苹果)正在为下一代消费电子设备使用 $SIVE 激光器。
信息发现:与 Lightelligence(已上市,市值 100 亿美元以上)+ Lightmatter 的关联,二者可能是客户。

Celestial(Celestial AI)放量,$MRVL(美满电子 / Marvell)给出指引信号。

新客户正在与 $SIVE 合作 TFLN(薄膜铌酸锂)方案,例如 Lightium。

$AMD(超微)选择 $GFS(格芯)做 CPO,而 GFS 将 Sivers 列为两家激光供应商之一。

Ayar 从官网上撤下了 $MTSI(MACOM)/ $LITE(Lumentum),并把 $SIVE 标示为主要供应商 / 独家供应源。

Ayar 为放量爬坡融资 5 亿美元(英特尔、联发科、英伟达、AMD 等参与)。
2025 年年报释出可插拔(pluggable)TAM(总潜在市场)扩张的信号。

预计很快在纳斯达克(Nasdaq)上市。

被纳入 MSCI 小盘指数 / Nasdaq OMX 指数,使贝莱德(BlackRock)、先锋(Vanguard)等成为被动买入者。
2025 年年报释出并购信号 + 两位拥有该领域经验的新董事会成员加入。

$NOK(诺基亚)在 2025 年年报中显示为可能的客户。
$LITE 因 EML 合同导致 CW 产能瓶颈,而 $SIVE 释出已与稳懋(WIN Semiconductor)达成产能协议的信号,使其有望成为 CPO 板块的瓶颈所有者 + 咽喉控制点(chokepoint)。

以上所有市场研究都是在财报发布之前完成的。

任何财报结果都只是对已完成的供应链映射的确认而已。

我认为没人会在乎上一季度的营收数字,因为 $SIVE 是一只极具吸引力的、押注 2027 年(尤其是下半年起)的多头标的。

我现在唯一关注的事情有:

整体光子学超级周期(photonics supercycle)的 TAM 扩张(例如光学引擎、ELS、可插拔模块),无论是来自并购还是技术发展。

现有公司的放量爬坡预期。

纳斯达克上市时间表,以便为公司的并购努力提供更多流动性支撑。
任何关于 CPO / 可插拔领域释出的新客户信号。

Addressing the disinformation: I haven’t sold and don’t plan to sell a single share. 

I do think this ends up the next $80B+ $LITE one day from ~$2.1B.

And I personally have plans to acquire more ownership + support https://t.co/Mb5eRuL2N7

text:
Been doing a lot of reading on the supply chain this year. I am not always right. But the ones that were right, were very right.

$IQE: 17p → 48 (only independent epiwafer foundry globally, photonics supply chain bottleneck)

$SOI: €70 → €192 (silicon photonics substrate near monopoly for AI data centers)

$SIVE: SEK 30 → SEK 70 (CPO laser source, InP chokepoint in the AI optical stack)

$ENSI: 72p → 116p (ASIC design house, AI data centers + Space + Post-Quantum)

$LBIRD: €19→ €26.5 (European defense laser leader, NATO rearmament cycle)

$CIFR: $15 → $25 (Bitcoin miner transitioning to AI data center operator, $9.3B contracted)

$ONDS: $8 → $13.6 (autonomous drone prime contractor, +1,065% Q1 YoY revenue)

$LPK: €18 → €24 (only proven glass substrate LIDE process globally, AI packaging)

None of these were obvious at entry. All had real risks. Most were ignored.

The market repricing confirms the reading.

text:
Looks like we agree on the similarities between $CPSH and $SIVE when it was just starting to break out...

Completely different businesses, but both are phenomenal chokepoint plays.

Back when $SIVE was sitting at ~8 SEK, most people saw it as just another small-cap photonics name.

Little did they know, it was hugely undervalued as a supplier sitting at a genuine bottleneck in the optical interconnect space... exactly the layer AI needed as data movement became the constraint.

The market eventually caught on and it proceeded to ~10x.

$CPSH feels like it's at that same pre-recognition stage, sitting at the same valuation as $SIVE before it took off.

> $SIVE: photonics/optical interconnects for AI datacenters

> $CPSH: AlSiC thermal management for defense, space, and potentially AI chips

Both are critical suppliers that get overlooked until market demand forces the re-rating.

And remember, $CPSH is the only meaningful US-based vertically integrated AlSiC supplier, with sole-source status on many applications per their own 10-K...

When the market starts to recognize this as a true bottleneck, it'll re-rate hard and fast.

$SIVE already showed how that plays out.

text:
When the energy foundation of a formation becomes this volatile, it signals the formation is no longer self-sustaining. The thermal combustion era is not ending slowly — it is convulsing.
Third, Democracy-as-productive-force under geopolitical stress. The US-Iran war is the Democracy column acting out its terminal contradiction: military power deployed to defend the thermal supply chain, while the same democracy-capital system is funding the AI/compute transition that makes that thermal supply chain obsolete. JPMorgan argues that accelerating oil inventory depletion will ultimately force the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz — possibly in June  (CNBC) . Even resolution confirms the diagnosis: the old formation can only survive by emergency management of its own energy base.
FARWATERS SIGNALS
In the context of the matrix, the following moves are formational signals, not just price action:
Marvell at +32% on Nvidia photonics partnership is the advanced packaging / optical interconnect farwater activating. This maps directly to Hanmi Semiconductor's territory: CoWoS, advanced packaging, interconnect density — the hardware layer of the AI productive force.
Navitas Semiconductor soaring on a partnership with Nvidia, with its 800V-to-6V DC-DC power delivery board displayed at the Nvidia showcase  (CNBC) — this is the power management / new energy efficiency farwater. Navitas operates in the same column as VINA TECH supercapacitors: making compute energy-efficient is how the new formation solves its own energy equation without thermal combustion dependency.
Coherent getting Nvidia backing for AI datacenter optics expansion is the compute-efficiency signal. Leeno Industrial and Openedges Technology's logic — test sockets for advanced chips, AI IP for efficiency — follows the same directional arrow.
RISKS BY THE MATRIX
The thermal supply shock risk remains the single largest formational risk. The World Bank projects Brent averaging $95–$115 in 2026 under current disruption scenarios — 10 to 35 percent above baseline  (World Bank) . This is a direct tax on the old formational capital base and a potential trigger for capital reversal from growth assets if the shock deepens unexpectedly.
Geopolitical containment risk is secondary: any escalation that draws China into the Hormuz equation — as the largest buyer of Gulf crude — creates a multi-formation crisis that no single policy instrument can contain.
AI capex reversal risk is the new formation's own vulnerability. Nvidia has declined in 7 of the last 10 sessions  (https://t.co/tF181fPTDi) , signaling that even the new formational flagship is subject to capital rotation when macro fear spikes. NVDA ex-dividend today (June 4, $0.25) is a minor technical event, but the pattern of high-volume selling into strength warns that Capitalist subjects are still booking profits in the transition zone — not yet fully committed to the new paradigm.
VERDICT: ACCELERATION SCORE — HIGH
Today's configuration — thermal combustion convulsing at $96 oil, embodied intelligence infrastructure being built at record speed by Nvidia/Marvell/Navitas, and capital visibly migrating between formations — represents one of the highest-velocity transition moments the matrix has recorded.
The farwaters are not waiting for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen. The new formational productive forces are being built because the old energy base is unstable. That is precisely the dynamic Pulatov's matrix predicts: the new formation does not replace the old at peak stability — it emerges through the crisis of the old.

text:
US AFTERMARKET + MACRO RISK SCAN — June 4, 2026
Matrix Analyst AI | Pulatov Matrix Framework
OVERVIEW: THE SCHIZOPHRENIC MARKET
The US market is caught in the most vivid illustration of the matrix's central thesis: two formational forces pulling simultaneously in opposite directions. On one side — Nvidia, valued at $5 trillion, trading around $221–232 range intraday on June 3  (Robinhood) , with the AI/compute mega-cycle accelerating. On the other — WTI futures at $96.02 and Brent at $97.81, rising as the US and Iran traded new strikes, with the Strait of Hormuz still partially blocked  (CNBC) . This is not a "mixed market." This is the rodovye skhvatki formatsii — the birth pangs of the transition, playing out in real time on the ticker.
OLD FORMATION COLUMN: THERMAL COMBUSTION UNDER SIEGE
The thermal energy base of late-stage Capitalism is experiencing its most acute stress test since 2008. Roughly 14 million barrels per day of oil supply remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with around 850 million barrels of cumulative supply lost over the first two months of the conflict  (ING THINK) . This is not a "geopolitical risk premium" — this is a structural rupture in the energy column of the old formational matrix.
Brent peaked at $138 per barrel in April — the highest since 2008 — before pulling back to the mid-$90s on ceasefire optimism, creating a violent $90–$100 range with a $138 tail-risk scenario if the strait fully closes  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) . Saudi Aramco's CEO has reportedly warned the market won't normalize until 2027 if the strait stays shut past mid-June. Capital is pricing a reopening that hasn't happened. That is the definition of fragile.
The Democracy-Capitalist subject is responding predictably: global oil output expected to fall 6.9 million barrels per day year-on-year in Q2 2026, the largest quarterly decline since COVID-19  (World Bank) . Professional union rights — the freedom level of the old formation — become irrelevant when thermal supply is collapsing. Inflation resets all social contracts.
NEW FORMATION COLUMN: AI/COMPUTE SIGNAL INTENSIFYING
While thermal combustion bleeds, the new productive force is printing record valuations. Nvidia at Computex announced Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot for academic research, DRIVE Hyperion as the global platform for a robotaxi-ready world, Alpamayo 2 Super Open Reasoning Model for robotaxis, and Cosmos 3 as the open frontier foundation model for physical AI  (CNN) — all in a single product cycle. This is not iteration. This is the embodied intelligence column of the new formational matrix going vertical.
Marvell Technology surged to $290.79, up 32%, after Nvidia CEO highlighted its AI infrastructure role  (Robinhood) . Marvell acquired Celestial AI — a provider of Photonic Fabric technology for next-generation scale-up interconnect — for $3.5 billion in February 2026, with the acquisition expected to accelerate connectivity strategy for next-generation AI and cloud data centers  (sec) . Photonics is the new energy efficiency layer: it sits in the "New energy / power management" column of the transition. This is not a trade — this is capital voting for the next formational architecture.
CONFLICTS OF TRANSITION
The matrix identifies three live collision zones right now:
First, thermal shock vs AI-boom dissociation. The same session saw oil near $96 and Nvidia near $232 all-time-high territory. The old formational capital (energy companies, industrial complex) is being taxed by the Hormuz crisis while new formational capital (AI, semiconductors, optics) breaks records. This is capital migrating between formations in real time.
Second, capital volatility as formational stress indicator. Brent ran from below $60 at the start of 2026 to a $138 April peak before the May unwind — a range that reflects the system repricing into an entirely different energy regime  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) .

Ticker: $LASE

The Board of Directors recommends that shareholders vote "FOR" the approval of both proposals to support the company's business plan and potential future financing.

text: Typical zionist manipulation today on photonics what a great day to buy more i added to my $aaoi position

2026 is shaping up to be the year of photonics and optical infrastructure. 🚀

🔹 $AAOI — Supplies optical transceivers powering AI data center connectivity. High-growth potential, but results can be volatile.

🔹 $AVGO — Dominates custom AI chips and networking hardware. One of the biggest beneficiaries of AI infrastructure spending.

🔹 $LITE — Leading provider of optical components for hyperscale data centers and telecom networks. Recovery story with AI tailwinds.

🔹 $CRDO — Builds high-speed connectivity solutions for AI clusters. Growing rapidly, though expectations are already sky-high.

🔹 $ALAB — Emerging player in optical networking silicon. Early innings with a massive addressable market ahead.

🔹 $GLW — The backbone behind fiber and specialty glass. Less flashy, but quietly benefits from the AI buildout.

🔹 $COHR — Photonics and laser technology leader. AI opportunity is significant, but recent integration challenges have masked the story.

🔹 $AEHR — Semiconductor testing specialist expanding beyond EVs into AI, GaN, and next-generation chip markets.

As AI scales, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as computing it. That’s where photonics comes in. 💡

text:
$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade.

"$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. text:
$STM at a new all-time high after hours. 🥂

The craziest part is this hasn’t hit the mainstream yet. 

STM is quietly ramping high-volume production of its PIC100 silicon photonics platform (800G and 1.6T optical transceivers) for hyperscaler AI clusters, with plans to quadruple capacity by 2027￼ backed by long-term customer reservations. 

The anchor underneath it all is a multi-year, multi-billion dollar AWS engagement ￼spanning connectivity, microcontrollers, and power ICs, which even came with warrants to AWS for up to 24.8 million ST shares ￼ that vest as AWS buys product.

AFTER-HOURS MOVERS — June 2, 2026  The after-hours session delivered extreme volatility as usual, with several low-float stocks making massive moves on news, earnings, or momentum.   Top After-Hours Gainers:  $XOS (Xos, Inc.) — +132.29%  
$YMAT (J-Star Holding) — +32.97%  
$VRAX (Virax Biolabs) — +20.20%  
$MI (NFT Limited) — +18.00%  
$LASE (Laser Photonics) — +17.77%

 Top After-Hours Losers:  $JZ (Jianzhi Education) — -22.86%  
$NRXP (NRx Pharmaceuticals) — -22.08%  
$MYND (https://t.co/azHZg4Qaxk) — -17.17%  
$ELOG — -16.31%  
$YEXT — -15.68%

Key Takeaway:
Today’s after-hours action was dominated by micro-cap and small-cap names with very low floats, leading to exaggerated percentage moves. $XOS stands out with an incredible 132% surge — likely driven by major news or contract announcement.  These kinds of moves are high-risk, high-reward and often reverse quickly at the open. Always check volume, catalysts, and news before jumping in.  Trading Advice:  Use limit orders and tight risk management  
Many of these names have extremely low liquidity  
Watch for continuation or reversal in pre-market tomorrow

text: @ByrneHobart Photonics have already been discovered

حفلة اليوم برعاية سهم $LASE 🔥🔥

Laser Photonics Corporation

▪️ارتفاع ( 160% في يوم واحد ) 🚀

▪️الأسباب الرئيسية لانفجار السهم:

✅ تم اختيار نظام مكافحة الدرونز ضمن برنامج حكومي دفاعي ( أهم خبر ) 🔥
✅ شراء عضو مجلس إدارة من السوق المفتوح قبل شهر 32,000 سهم
✅ طلبية من Johnson & Johnson 
✅ قيمة سوقية صغيرة جداً (~80 مليون دولار)

❌ لا يوجد حتى الآن عقد رسمي من وزارة الدفاع
❌ طلبية J&J صغيرة نسبياً (250 ألف دولار فقط)

▪️نشاطها : 
- أنظمة الليزر الصناعية
- إزالة الصدأ والتنظيف بالليزر
- أنظمة دفاعية مضادة للدرونز (LSAD)
- تطبيقات طبية عبر شركة CMS Laser التابعة لها

▪️الخلاصة : 

السوق لا يسعر ما حدث اليوم فقط…
بل احتمال أن تتحول شركة صغيرة قيمتها 80 مليون دولار إلى شركة دفاعية تمتلك عقوداً حكومية مستقبلاً

ملاحظة : السهم غير شرعي ، فقط مشاركة معلومات للسهم

text:
水曜朝ニュース（6/3 JST）

AIデータセンター、GPUだけ見てると次を取り逃がしそう。

$NVDA はVera Rubinで tokens per MW を前面に。$MRVL は「次のボトルネックはconnectivity」と整理。NVIDIAもSpectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsで電力効率5倍を打ち出した。

TrendForceは2027年HBM4契約価格の大幅上昇観測。$MU には追い風。

次は光I/O、HBM、電力・冷却、先進パッケージング。$LITE $COHR $VRT $TSM も見る。

text:
마벨(Marvell) CEO, "구리의 벽(Copper Wall)이 랙 내부로 이동하고 있으며, 이를 해결할 유일한 방법은 공동 패키징 광학(CPO)뿐"이라고 밝혀

*마벨 테크놀로지(Marvell Technology)의 CEO 맷 머피(Matt Murphy)는 "AI 인프라의 다음 주요 병목 현상은 연산(Compute)이나 메모리가 아니라, 바로 연결성(Connectivity)

*머피 CEO는 구리(Copper)에서 광학(Optical) 인터커넥트로의 전환이 먼 미래가 아닌 '지금 당장' 일어나고 있으며, 이는 반도체 산업 역사상 전례가 없을 정도로 거대한 수요의 물결을 일으킬 것이라고 말했음.

*구리의 벽(Copper Wall)의 물리학

- 신호가 구리 케이블을 통해 이동할 수 있는 거리는 대역폭에 반비례

- 레인당 100Gbps 수준: 구리 케이블은 약 5미터까지 도달할 수 있어 하나의 랙(Rack) 내부에서 서버들을 연결하기에 충분.

- ​레인당 200Gbps 수준: 그 거리는 약 2.5미터로 줄어들며, 이는 랙의 높이와 내부 라우팅 요구 사항을 감안할 때 딱 한계치에 해당.

- ​레인당 400Gbps 수준: 현재 업계가 나아가고 있는 이 단계에서는 구리선으로 랙 내부를 완전히 연결하는 것이 더 이상 불가능.

*구리의 벽이 한 단계 이동할 때마다, 광학으로 전환해야 하는 연결의 수는 최소 10배 이상 증가. 머피 CEO는 이를 광학 산업의 '수요 폭발'로 묘사.

*​공동 패키징 광학(CPO): 왜 필요한가?

- 랙 내부에서의 전환은 랙과 랙 사이(Inter-rack)에 사용되는 기존의 광학 기술로는 해결할 수 없다. 랙 내부의 연결 수는 랙 간 연결 수보다 약 10배 더 많음. 이 정도의 밀도에서 표준 광학 모듈을 장착하는 것은 전력과 공간 부족으로 인해 물리적으로 불가능.

- ​CPO는 광 엔진(Optical Engines)을 스위치나 연산 패키지에 직접 통합함으로써 이 문제를 해결. 광섬유를 전면 패널이 아닌 칩(Chip)으로 직접 가져오는 방식. 이 기술은 최첨단 CMOS 공정, 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh), 첨단 패키징, 그리고 광 인터커넥트를 하나의 긴밀하게 통합된 시스템으로 결합.

- 엔비디아(Nvidia)의 베라 루빈(Vera Rubin) 플랫폼이 이미 생산 단계의 첫 CPO 기반 스위치인 '스펙트럼-X 이더넷 포토닉스(Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics)'를 도입했다는 점을 언급하며, 이러한 전환이 더 이상 이론에 머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시.

*오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨.

*​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨.

*머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조.

머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시. *오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨. *​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨. *머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조. https://t.co/VkQZnIMKUB"}}}, "rest_id": "2061919430054117501", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "382", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
AI depends on physical infrastructure: chips, photonics, optical interconnects and data centers. Our new article explores where amorphous fluoropolymers may fit in the AI value chain.

https://t.co/qMKbgEzsSR

#AI #Photonics #Materials

Vad jag vet har Sivers aldrig haft något PM med GF inom Photonics. Så egentligen är det första gången det blir officiellt. Men så klar,  kunde man haft med en notis och att man varit ecosystem partners på olika sätt. Dock är det nog nedan OCI MSA partners (som inte heller nämn), som egentligen gör nyheten extra intressant.

Researchers have built a miniaturized microcomb-driven terahertz wireless system that delivers record-breaking speeds topping 100 Gbps at ultrahigh frequencies. The entire system is 90 times smaller than conventional terahertz chips making it finally practical for real-world use. Why this matters: • This is one of the biggest technical hurdles for true 6G: generating and controlling terahertz waves efficiently in tiny devices • 100+ Gbps at these frequencies could enable holographic video calls, massive real-time AI, and instant global connectivity • The photonic microcomb approach turns light into the engine for future wireless networks far more efficient than traditional electronics The deeper implication is enormous: We may be watching the birth of photonic 6G. Instead of struggling with bulky electronics at extreme frequencies, future networks could run on light itself inside chips small enough to fit in your phone or even a satellite. Coherent $COHR – Nvidia invested $2 billion for fiber optic transceivers and photonics to connect GPU clusters

CoreWeave $CRWV – Nvidia invested $2 billion to help build out AI factories

Lumentum $LITE – Nvidia put $2 billion toward advanced laser and optical networking products

Marvell Technology $MRVL – Nvidia invested $2 billion for custom networking and silicon photonics connectivity

Nebius Group $NBIS – Nvidia put $2 billion into this Dutch company for large scale AI cloud infrastructure

OpenAI – Nvidia committed $30 billion to help build out compute infrastructure

text:
A lot of people also misunderstand, half of that pipeline is Wireless which will ramp and come online first in 2027 while the latest move up is almost purely Photonics. Per CEO on latest ER call “the numbers (on the two) are now nearly at par”.

Real Photonics production revenue won’t occur until late 2028 at the earliest.

🚨 $CRDO reports Monday and Wall Street is about to find out it's been pricing the wrong company. 👀

They still see a copper-cable vendor.

What they're missing: a full-stack AI connectivity platform growing ~6x in two years that just bought its way into silicon photonics.

The numbers Monday:
→ ~$1.03 EPS (+194% YoY)
→ ~$430M revenue (+155% YoY)
→ FY26 revenue TRIPLING off FY25

But the print isn't even the real story.

🔭 The DustPhotonics deal changes everything. 🔥

$CRDO just closed a $750M acquisition that takes it vertical - copper to optical, chip to cluster. SerDes → DSP → silicon photonics → full system, all in-house. The two things that bottleneck AI at scale are power and reliability, and Credo now owns the stack that solves both across BOTH copper and optical.

Management's own number: the optical portfolio alone is targeted to do 💥 $500M+ in revenue in FY2027 into a silicon-photonics market headed for ~$6B by 2030.

That's not in the model. That's not in the multiple. That's the re-rate hiding in plain sight. 👀

Here's the tell: last quarter they crushed it ($1.07 vs ~$0.85, revenue +201%) and the stock still dropped ~15%. The bar is set at perfection. So every time the reactive crowd sells a clean print, patient capital accumulates the platform underneath.

⚠️ It's priced rich - a binary print into a premium chart cuts both ways. But that's the price of admission for owning a category winner before the category is obvious.

The market's still trading the cable.

I'm holding the platform. Long, and not for the trade. Long $CRDO 🔥
